purpose: Register of passage PAIRS in one long nonfiction argument-shaped work that address the SAME question Q (LLM-judge), each pair carrying a neutral interrogative Q and both passages' verbatim loci (doc, start_char, end_char, quote), emitted in DOCUMENT ORDER. It asserts NO relation between the paired passages — not agreement, not conflict, not contradiction, not tension, not which passage is right. The model only points at two passages that share a question; the HUMAN reads both and owns 100% of the conflict call. This is the deliberate NON-step across the content-verdict wall (the task is split so the model cannot assert opposition). Two mechanical, posture-critical gates carry the firewall: the F4 Q-string gate (a surfaced question must be interrogative in FORM and free of relation vocabulary — a case-folded substring scan — else the pair is REFUSED, counted, and disclosed; the form check is SYNTAX-ONLY and gives zero protection against loaded/presuppositional questions, so the human terminus is the guarantee) and the F3 runtime banned-key walk (RAISES on any relation key anywhere in the envelope, or any generic verdict key inside results.pairs, before the envelope is returned). Pair caps (default 12/question, 60/work, operator-tunable) are a DISCLOSURE: over-cap survivors are the first by document order and dropped loci are logged — never a tension/confidence ranking. Uncalibrated; no run-to-run determinism guarantee for live backends (mock/manifest are deterministic by construction). Register scope v1: nonfiction-argument only (declared in the claim surface, not gated). Anchors: ContraDoc (arXiv:2311.09182) contradiction-type taxonomy; BeliefShift (arXiv:2603.23848) position-drift framing.
use_when: You have one long nonfiction argument-shaped work and want a human-facing register of passages that address the same question, as a reading aid (you decide any conflict)
You want passage pairs surfaced relation-free, with verbatim loci, so a human can check whether an author's stated position drifts across a work
You have an LLM judge (or a pre-computed pair manifest) and want the deterministic mock/manifest backend for CI
do_not_use_when: You want the tool to decide whether two passages conflict, contradict, or which is right (it refuses — the register asserts no relation; the human owns that call)
You are working on fiction or a narrator/character's beliefs (v1 is nonfiction-argument register only; a fiction arc can license a stated change, making false positives severe)
You need an exhaustive contradiction sweep or a clean bill of health (absence of a pair is NOT evidence of consistency)

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, --out PATH, --out-md PATH

compute.cost_note: LLM judge per doc (per-work pairing) via Anthropic / OpenAI / Gemini; free with --judge manifest (pre-computed pairs); key-free with --judge agent_host (delegates to the host runtime's model). The mock backend is deterministic and CI-safe.

examples.description: Position-pair register with pre-computed pairs (JSON envelope)
Direct API spot-check, markdown report to a file

references: ContraDoc: Understanding Self-Contradictions in Documents with Large Language Models (Li, Raheja & Kumar), arXiv:2311.09182: https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.09182
BeliefShift (opinion-drift / belief-consistency benchmark), arXiv:2603.23848 (sweep-sourced id, Mar 2026): https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.23848
